task_completion.description: Divide the open shell into four distinct functional zones and label them clearly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan shows four distinct areas labeled as Retail Showroom, Repair Workshop, Parts Storage Room, and Breakroom/Restroom (or very similar variations).

task_completion.description: Use interior partition walls and connecting doors to separate the zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Interior walls are drawn inside the main building shell to separate the rooms, and doors are placed within these walls to allow access between the zones.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Retail Showroom with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Retail Showroom area, there are at least 6 display shelves/cabinets, a checkout desk, 1 chair, and 4 bicycles (or 4 additional shelves if bicycles are not used).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Repair Workshop with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Repair Workshop area, there are at least 3 workbenches (or large tables) and 4 tool storage units (cabinets/shelves).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Breakroom/Restroom with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Breakroom/Restroom area, there is a sofa or lounge chair, at least one appliance (e.g., refrigerator, microwave), a toilet, and a sink.

task_completion.description: Place at least 30 total furniture items and save the file as bike_shop.sh3d.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The furniture list panel shows a dense list of items (visually appearing to be 30+ total across all rooms), and the SweetHome3D window title or a save dialog shows the filename 'bike_shop.sh3d'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SweetHome3D graphical user interface to design the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SweetHome3D application open with the 2D plan, 3D view, and furniture catalog being actively used, rather than a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent built the layout inside the provided building shell.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exterior walls of the original starter file (bicycle_shop_starter.sh3d) are still present, and the new layout is built within them, rather than in a completely blank new project.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the design process by running a pre-written script to generate the save file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows progressive placement of walls, doors, and furniture in the GUI over time.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using SweetHome3D to modify 'bicycle_shop_starter.sh3d'. The agent must create walls, place doors, define rooms, and place specific furniture. Note: If bicycles are unavailable in the catalog, the agent is allowed to substitute them with additional shelves. Due to a known logic overlap, shelves placed in the layout might visually serve as both bicycle substitutes and tool storage units; accept shelves as valid substitutes for these items if placed in the appropriate rooms. The pass threshold is 70/100.
